name: matrix_security
description: Security awareness: secret detection and redaction, firewall rule analysis.

episodes.description: Identify and redact multiple types of secrets embedded in application logs.

steps.name: redact_secrets
steps.input:
Scan these application logs and redact ALL secrets/credentials. Replace each secret value with [REDACTED], preserving the surrounding text.

2026-03-28 08:01:12 INFO  Starting application server on port 8080
2026-03-28 08:01:13 DEBUG Connecting to database: postgresql://appuser:S3cret!Pass@db.internal:5432/myapp
2026-03-28 08:01:14 INFO  Database connection established
2026-03-28 08:01:15 DEBUG AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
2026-03-28 08:01:15 DEBUG AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY
2026-03-28 08:01:16 INFO  S3 bucket connected: my-app-data
2026-03-28 08:01:17 INFO  User auth service started
2026-03-28 08:01:18 DEBUG Session token: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.dozjgNryP4J3jVmNHl0w5N_XgL0n3I9PlFUP0THsR8U
2026-03-28 08:01:19 INFO  Health check endpoint at /health
2026-03-28 08:01:20 INFO  Using API key: sk-proj-abc123def456ghi789jkl012mno345pqr678
2026-03-28 08:01:21 DEBUG GitHub token: ghp_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdef12
2026-03-28 08:01:22 INFO  Worker pool initialized (4 threads)
2026-03-28 08:01:23 INFO  Application ready

Reply as strict JSON with keys:
- redacted_log: the full log with secrets replaced by [REDACTED] (keep all non-secret text exactly as-is)
- secrets_found: array of {"type": "<kind of secret>", "line_number": <N>}

Do NOT include actual secret values anywhere in your response. No markdown, just JSON.

episodes.description: Analyze iptables rules for security issues, duplicates, and ordering problems.

steps.name: audit_firewall
steps.input:
Audit these iptables rules for security issues:

*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p icmp -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 192.168.1.0/24 -j ACCEPT
-A OUTPUT -j ACCEPT
COMMIT

Reply as strict JSON:
- critical_issues: array of strings (immediate security risks)
- warnings: array of strings (concerning but not critical)
- redundant_rules: array of strings (duplicates or unreachable rules)
- recommendation: one paragraph with the most important changes

No markdown, just JSON.

checks.rubric: Must identify: (1) CRITICAL: '-A INPUT -j ACCEPT' (blanket accept all inbound) makes all subsequent rules unreachable. (2) SSH port 22 is open to all IPs without rate limiting. (3) SSH rule is duplicated. (4) The port 8080 rule restricted to 192.168.1.0/24 is unreachable because blanket ACCEPT comes before it. Recommendation should include: remove blanket ACCEPT, set default DROP policy for INPUT, restrict SSH, remove duplicate rules, reorder so restricted rules are reachable.
